title: Garlicky Juicy German Sauerbraten Stew
description: A hearty, garlicky German stew made with beef, vinegar, and spices, perfect for a cozy dinner.

ingredients.name: beef (3 pound top round or rump roast)

instructions: In a large bowl, combine beef, onion, garlic, apple, cumin, mustard seeds, salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
Pour vinegar and beef broth over the beef mixture, and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 days or up to 4 days.
Remove the beef mixture from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Transfer the beef mixture to a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in brown sugar.
Let the stew cool slightly, then serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
Discard any excess fat that rises to the surface.
